speaker: Sam Schrager
words: This conversation with Mike Stefanos took place at the New York Shoe Shine Parlor on Main Street in Lewiston on September 1, 1976. There was a considerable amount of street noise on this tape, and for the first half hour the fan in the shop was on, making it rather more difficult to understand what was being said.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: How old were you when you came over here?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So what year was it?

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Would you tell us what it was like growing up where you grew up there? Where? Near Poros?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: All. Nothing was growing up, you know, just working people. Nothing growing up. In the little villages where I come from, they produce lots of lemons. They got lots of lemon trees there, you know. They produce lots of lemons. And they produce lots of olives.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: You understand now?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Olives. You make the olives all. And they produce some grapes.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And they got real art for the art of which not too much.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: A little what?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: For each guy they got two, three, four lemons.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: So were the people poor?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: No. The people was. And they had something, you know, a party or something to work for, you know. They didn't come on this court. But here the country is good. So that's it. And we come on this court. We come back in the morning and go back in there. And we come over here and we say, okay.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: So when you first thought about coming, you figured you'd come back?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yeah.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: In your family when you grew up, did you have to worry about having enough to eat?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: We never worried. You know back there people lived with olives.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And lots of dandelions. Bread. No worries. Wine. And milk. They drink wine, estate, coffee, all worry.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Oh my, for me, when I eat, oh not very often. Only when they are some holidays. They go on this court, you know, they got, oh and they kill some in a little town there, you know. They got little bushes, you know. And they kill some lamb, goats, you know, something like that. Not big.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So, but when we working on a go down the field. Working on a grape.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Or olives. Or lemon. Only we take with us for lunch.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Little bread, slash bread. Sometimes fresh, sometimes dry.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And cheese, you know. And if you can afford it, you know, you get little bottle of wine with you.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: What do you eat? What do you need?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Nothing much.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: In the evening, on a dinner, the mother, you know, them I said like now in the summertime, they got gardens, you know, they got gardens. While they cooking some string beans. Or spinach. Or cauliflower. Or celery, you know, something like that. No meat. Meat we eat.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Like Thanksgiving. Or Christmas. Then meat. Yeah. Or New Year. Except if you got a few children, you know, your children, then you can kill one of your children. And have them near. You can roast them. Or you can make a stew. And put some macaroni in them. Or something else. And have them with good meat. We never worry for anything. Because we rest without, you know, olives, dry bread, orange, cheese.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: In your family, did your mother work outside of the house?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: No, at home. Stay home. Except, she's working during, when they harvest the grapes. Or the lemons. This time you know. Now they know for sure they're gonna start cut the lemons. Cut the lemons. Let the mother go and help them. Or she can do something else around. Or some neighbors. They might need really help. They call them. She go. Help them. Or sometimes she get dollar a day. Something like that.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Get what?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Dollar a day. She dollar a day back there then. Was, uh, pretty good money. But now, everything is pretty high over there. High living. Like they are here today.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: What did your father do for a living?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well. My father,

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: when he was doing the same thing. Working in the, here, there. And then 19,

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: then,

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: I don't remember. But they came from ....

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: He did?

speaker: Speaker 2
words: Yeah.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: He came from .... Then he was in California. He was working in Arado.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Working on what?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Arado. Uh huh. Ushabi. Yes. Well, he stay, you might say three, four, six, I don't know. And he come home 19, 12. Between 12 or 14. Something like that. I don't remember exactly.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well, then, he bring little money, you know. And he went and he invest the little money he brought with him. From here. And bought a little coffee house, you know. Ushabi. Back there, you know, they got, same time, you know, they sell really coffee. But the most of it. They got little lucky, they got here a bill power. Back there, you know, they got to, they got really coffee, but most of it, they sell lots of wine for the people, you know, before they go. And they drink. Or they have a little fun, you know. And he was left pretty good living with that. So he did pretty good over here to make enough money to buy that there. Yes, but I remember this. Them days, them, one United States dollar was five drachmas over there. And there was a lot of money there, you know. So it was worth more than a dollar over there. Yes. And he was make up pretty good. And besides that, we had to, you know, not too much, make his own weed, you know, to spend, to last for year, till next year, you know. Sometimes they last, sometimes they go and buy, plow and make bread. Then they take homemade bread. They do them homemade bread. They don't buy bread.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And I got few, three, four acres of bread, you know, will learn. Bread patch, good patching breads. And some lemon, about hundred trees of lemon. You're showing, lemon. And we had about hundred, no, hundred and fifty olive trees. This was all on the place you grew up on.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Your father owned?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yes.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Was this before he came to America or?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yes. He always owned it. But it wasn't enough to make a good living. No. See, he can make a living before, and that's why he came in the United States. He stayed three, four years. I don't remember.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Did he come with a bunch of friends?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yes.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Did they stick together when they were over here?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well, when they come over here, you know, it's pretty hard, everybody, you know. But he was in California. He was at the Sacramento, California, in Oroville, California. And then there, you know, there was lots of people around, lots of Greece, lots of people, you know, from the same town. And they're working at a rate of an extra gain, you know.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: They go by fine. After he got to leave, the money come home. But then, you know, he was living a little bit nothing, you know, cheap living, like he was here during the depression. But now, back there, it's offline, like they are here.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: What did he think? Did he talk to you about the living conditions over here for the guys on the extra gain and the railroad work?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Oh, hell yes. What did he... He says, if you go back there, you're going to work. No fool around. Work ten hours a day. Work outside in a truck, you know. Sound. Too hot, too windy at the time. Friend. You. You are the one. When I came on this phone, 1914, I came with two friends of mine from the same town, neighbors, you know. One of them, he was on the United States, he had two trips coming and going, coming and going in the United States. But he never stayed very long. The first time when he came, he stayed two, three years and he come home. And then he stayed back there, I don't know how long, and he come back again. And then he come home again. And the time when I come, he come back again. See. Well, he always, time when he was here in the United States, he always go to California. Always, you know, he was working on Sacramento. They know lots of Greeks in there, you know. And they know guy from the old town, and he was a sexier for them, a gang for them. And it was easy for them to get a job, you know. And one of them, he was a superintendent. And that's where they was given easy job, you know. My wickie turned out today. Well, we come together to New York.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And from New York, we stayed two, three days there, and then we came to Chicago.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well, that guy who was coming to go two, three times, he had a brother home, you know. Outside from Chicago, in the big factories, you know, big...

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Steel?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Steel factory. He was working there. He was about 35 miles, I guess, from Chicago, something like that. I don't know. So he called him up, and he come to Chicago, and he meet us, you know. Well, we are mistake of the days. He says, where you go, where you go, and when you go. So his brother -in -law, he told him, you go to Sacramento with his friend. His friend, first time he came to this country like I did, see.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So he says, he asked me, he says, what you gonna do?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So I told him, I says, I got a... He knows them guys, you know, from the same town. I says, I wrote there to my friend at Patra Chaitagh, right there for Gus is long.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And he was working a sawmill. And I wrote him there. If I come over here. And I had another cousin in Sacramento, I wrote him there.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And this fellow who came, and he meet us, he asked me, he says, I know good friend of mine, and he got a barber shop. And he can put you in a shiny shoes, see. I say, pardon me, I think you're gonna make a very good morning. I says, okay, that's all right, you gotta work, you know. Now I don't know how he got guy, or he go back to working, he wrote me a letter, I guess, he did a letter delay, I don't know something wrong with the letter. And I didn't got it.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: But the same time, I had a wire from here, from Patra Chaitagh, from friend of mine, and he wire him a ticket, you know, to come here in Patra Chaitagh. At the same time, I had another one from California, and they asked me if I want to go to California, and said the ticket. Already I got the ticket from him. See, from here. So this fellow, who told me, he know a good friend, he got a barber shop, and he can put lead there, you know, put chair there, and start working chances. I didn't show up, and he come back to Chicago to find out what wrong, you know.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Where was his friend shop? Where was that gonna be?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Oh, on the place where he was working.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Oh, near Chicago?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yeah, outside from Chicago, you know, some of them factories, you know. There was this town there, I don't know the town, you know. Yeah, so he came back to... He come back to find out why he wrote me letter, and I didn't answer, and I didn't go there, you know, and he like to find out, you know. So I told him, I said, no, I never got any letter.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: You're okay?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Oh yeah, he says, I wrote to you, and I got a job for you. I said, yeah, but look here. I got wire, and a ticket, now I gotta go down. Union Pasheef to get the ticket, and go to Patra Chaitagh. One night, though.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Well, he says, you want to go west?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: I says, yeah, I gotta go over there. They got a job for me, only sewing.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well, I come to Patra Chaitagh. Them days that they got a good sewing mill, they're milling me. I was working ten hours a day, working nights. I'm milling working two days, two shifts a day, and I think ten hours a day. But anyway, I stay out, they work about three, four days, so Billy Foreman, he put new work there.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Where was you?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: I was working tonight.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And I was giving two dollars a day. Them days, you know, two dollars a day was lots of money, man. And we was four or five guys, you know, we rented a house, company, they had some houses, you know. But like it used to be company town, you know. And we was batching together, you know. We eat nice, eat everything we want. And them day, you know, everything like it. It was four or five, batching. And it was cost us eight, nine dollars a month in board room.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: A piece?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: A piece, yes. Them days, you do now. Now they do not. Of course, you only want the seven, eight dollars. You cook for yourselves. Yes.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Take turns?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yes. Each of us, you know, work, you cook. I was washing dishes. The other pala, we go to the store and buy things. What we're going to use, what we're going to need. The other pala, tonight, today, that's you, you are cook. Next day, he got to be the other guy. We had three cooks. They change every day. One, all the time he was there, he go buy things. And the other one might say, I want the dishwasher, all the time.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: All the time?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yes.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: You didn't like to cook?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well, I was young, you know. See, there was big guys, you know. I didn't know how to cook. Time went away, and the Gus, he come to ... in 15 minutes.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Who was your friend in ...?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: He's gone back home. I know he left from here, he went to California. I don't know if he stayed or died, I don't know.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: What was the job, the first job that you had in the village?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Oh, I had the first job I had. Nearly there was catching ants, you know. Catching ants, that's a little box, you know, inside there. And behind on them, so you know, on the, the balls came from carrots, you know. They got to go through the sauce, so they can take the eggs out of them, you know. So the clear ball go right down and go, they got a little table there. And go in the little table, from there, they got a sauce, you know. 10, 15, 20, 22, every two, every three, four, four, three. Then, uh, a little bit at a time. I always, you know, the boys, they like it changed. I said, well, I'm coming here and I tried it, yeah. I'm taking my place for a little while to learn this job. I said, sure. I said, it's okay. Two or three, you know. I come, get my job and I go to Greece. And little by little I learned that job there, and what I've seen, I learned that from Don Maru. Till the last, uh, when Miele Shida, 1931, I was a lumber director. Because I was hold there in a lumber all the time.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: That's a pretty good job, isn't it?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yeah, and it was paid pretty good money, the truth.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And after the mill, you know, during the Depression, she was jock and rocking. And I finally, I had a friend of mine over here and he had a shoe shank problem, you know. Down the stairs. And during the summertime, always that the mill they shut down, you know, for something repairing, you know, for weeks. Give the people a little vacation. No pay. No overtime. Straight time. And I always come down and visit him, he had a little shikai and he had nothing to do. So, you know, go up in the down the street, you know, I was a stranger. So, he'd phone me one day. Come on, here I show you how to shank seriously to go up in the down the street.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: You see anything? Do anything?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Come on, you can tell he said something. You might open your shop, like I did, see. So I got in. Really big every day, every day, so I could shank. Straight for a week, and then I went back to Sowmy. You know, when Sowmy shut down in 1931 in Okhbuk, I came down here. And I bought a place. I had a little money. And I bought a little fish and all the down the street.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: With half -ingles with another guy. So, I came from the...

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: to the 32th till... April, June, July, till April 1932. So, another fella come along from Potlatch. We work together. He like to get in the business.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And he told me one day, he said, you're only allowed to buy you out. He had more money than that guy, you see. I said, it's okay, I'll sell it to you. So I did sold my half -ingles to him. He had a partner.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So... I know from mine, he had a little farm outside by .... This... mile, mile and a half from the ....

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: You know who the ... is? The Klamath tradition, you know who the Klamath tradition is?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Right there, here a little... place there, couple of... three acres. And he was... produce then vegetables, you know. Green... carrots, beans... lettuce... vegetables, you know. Little bit of everything to make a living. Then they, you know, all them vegetables was... awful cheap! Only you can make a living by go till you know... live in us and produce, you know. You are slow, you can for a dollar, you know, you can go to the store and buy anything you want to, see. When I was here, this place here, with another guy, I went to store them, I buy for a dollar, I buy bread, I buy... pound of bread, I buy... eggs, I buy this meat too. But now you can't do that, you can buy now even a lot of bread, see.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: So you went to work for him?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: No, I just fool around with him. Oh yeah. See, because the time went.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So, another fella, I have from Potlatch's. He didn't have a job.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And one day, I had a little car, you know, and I went to Potlatch's to visit them boys. I didn't have nothing then, you know, I sold my hairpin, you see.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So one guy, he says... Why don't you look down there, you might find place, so we... open up... You know, we never expect to make money, but we can make a living, you know, stay to fool around, go here, go there. You can get a job then, you know. Them there, you can get a good job, no place.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And he says, I got the money, I told him, I says, I got much money. Well, he says, I got some, I put that much for my part.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And he says, well... Hi, I'm John. My phone! I'm having a more challenging trip. Yeah, he says... No problem, I put the rest of it. If you can find a place, so we open up a little church on the part. I come back here, and I used to stay out there on the friend mine, you know. She lived out there, you know, and I was helping them, you know, during the summertime, when we were vegetables. Now, this place here, there was a colored boy, he had this place here. And he was behind there in three months.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And he had not only a bank, but holding $150, something like that.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: The old?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: The old guy. So, I came and I asked him if he can sell out, yes, he says.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: He says, how much you want?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Oh, he says $600.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: I says, $600?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: That's too much money.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: If you're looking for business, that's all I want, $600. All right. I didn't say yes or no, you know.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: So, they told me, who owned this building?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And, how and, now the owner, the real owner, he was living here. And he had a store down there, where is now a barber shop. But, he had a friend of his, and he had a real estate. And he was the fella who collected the rent, and he rented that place. He was doing the business. He had to authority to sell, to collect the rent, or if he can get a little more rent, he can tell the boy, if you can pay that much rent, you move on. I guess somebody has to take a moment. So, I went and I talked to him, you know, and he says, now he says, I told my dad, he says, Mikey, now listen. Don't buy that place before you see me, before you talk to me now, but now a good thing you come and talk to me. Don't buy it. Because he is behind three months, and he also money in American bank. Prosecurity bank used to be American bank then.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: I said, okay. I like to buy him, but he asked me for too much money.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: He said, how much money he want?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: I said, $600. And $600, I said, too much money. And he got no, the chairs was no good. I said, if I buy that place, I have to throw everything out and buy new ones, you know.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well, he says, how much you, you want to pay?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well, I tell you, I says, I have, I give him $400. Because they going to cost me another three, four hundred dollars to buy equipment, you know, buy this and buy that. Because they, whatever he got in, I'm going to throw everything out. Don't go to nickel.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: He says, I'll buy it for you. I says, okay. He came and he talked to this guy, and he told me, he says, you're behind three months, you owe money to me. For, if you got any money to pay me, all right, or I got somebody else to buy this place.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well, that poor guy, he had no choice, you know. He says, he give up. Nothing he can do.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And he says, how much?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: He offered me, he says, $400. I said, pay your debts, pay the debts. He said, $75 to $100 to the good unit. Well, I said, okay.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well, I jump on my car and I went to the Potlatch again and I see that friend of mine, I told him, I got a place, I got such a club down. And I bought him for $400. Come on, let's go. He jump in the car, you know, we came down here. We go down there, the boss.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: I did this to him, my partner. We came and fixed the papers, you know, everything. So, that was July.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Last part of July. And we can get him all...

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: We can get up, that was Tuesday, Wednesday, something like that, when the whole weather closed that way. So, we can take him over, you know, Monday, Thursday, next week.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: We pay them, we pay them $250 here. We got the money. We pay the band, whatever they left, they give to this one. You know.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Was that this place?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yes. He was not his player, so this building belongs here. He had the business here, you know. He was pay rent, like I do. See, this building belongs to somebody else. And a list, you know. Pay so much, he come in. See. And he never did pay for three months, he was behind the rent. We were about a month from then. It was during the depression, you know, the morning, and everything.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Would it be okay with you if I shut off that fan for a little while?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: I'm having a hard time hearing some things you say. It would be easier to hear you. Well, when we close the deal, you know, we pay $400 there. Guy who was taking care of this business. To give the rent, you know, and this and that.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well, the panda of mine and I, whatever he had there, you know, we throw everything out. And he went to Spokane and he owned them churches, you sit there. We owned five churches. $18 a piece, them days. No, you can't touch them.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: We throw everything. Then, foot rest.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: They didn't have any. They have any some woods. Foot rest in here. We throw that out. And we sat in Chicago and went like this. We bought this salesman from Oregon Leather Company.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: We are giving him policy. He comes every month, you know. We sell policy, we sell strings, this and that.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So I asked him one day, I said, you know where we can get some foot rest?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Because we're going to throw out this goddamn thing. It's no good. He says, yes.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: He says, how many chairs you going to have and how many per one?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: I says, I'm going to have you. I said, I'll have you chair right over there. But I took him out for five years.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: I said, who are you going to buy?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: He says, I'm ordering some high -pass for you in Chicago. I'm going to cost you $12 a pair. It's pretty cheap then. Now you can buy them now from 12 dollars. Brushes then was 7, 8 dollars and 47, it's a job, 12, 14, 15 dollars. You can find brushes like you have them there. It's a different thing.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Were people having their shoes shined during the depression?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yes. Not too many. But we just get enough money to go buy. You see, pay us. Then, lights, newspaper, polish. And we had a little shack, you know. Right. Three, four blocks above. And we asked, batching. Ourselves, go to store buy anything we have. And in the end of the month we might, they give up, so about 10 dollars, 15 dollars, 8 dollars.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Who were the people that were coming in to have their shoes shined?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: All the people, that goes.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: It wasn't just the well -to -do people?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: No, no, no, no, no. Working people, they never did get a shine. All the, I tell you right now, I got the most of the business from outside people.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: You understand?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: These people now, businessmen. They don't get shine like they used to them days.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Few people I know, they got a business, they can't come and give shine once. Every, by say, two months. Maybe longer than that. But my business, I got some friends around, you know, some attorneys, some doctors, you know, some. They get shine but not very often, you know, like they did not like them days. But you say even in them days the wealthy people didn't come for their shoe shine.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: What did you do?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well, like these store managers, you know, they make a pretty good morning, you know, they never did shine their shoes. They don't care how they look like. They shine themselves, you know.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: You're sorry?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yeah. I don't know why. Like I told you, I got more business from outside. Like the working people, like you and somebody else. The rest of the people, they shine his own shoes these days because they got some goddamn cheap palaces, you know, spray, you know. A lot of people, you know, they don't have shoes, they use the goddamn palaces. They don't have shoes, they don't know how to do.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: And sometimes they bring in patients and they say, you want me to take that stuff out and clean them up and shine them up?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: I say, no, you put this on and I say, you take them out. No, I don't. I say, all right, I can do it. You do what you say.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Did you think at the time that you might go back to Potlatch after the Depression?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: No, because I had dysplasia. And I was my own boss. And I was making pretty good damn business, picking up little bit.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Did it keep picking up as the 30s went on? Did business keep getting better?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yes, after 37, 38. But after 40, 42, 44, 45, then. During the war I had three girls walking in here.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Really?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yes, shine your shoes, three girls. I had five chairs. I put these chairs on them chairs that they last me till 1948.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: 1942, when it kind of wear out, you know, and I know a friend down here. They got a shoe store and I went down there and he had some, you know, like this.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: So I told him, can you, can't you order me five chairs like this?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: He says, yes. But they are going to take a week. I got ordered in Chicago. I said, I can order a week, they have two weeks. So he did order some, you know. Cost me about $12 a week. And I took them out, you know, and I couldn't use them. But three girls working, there must have been a lot of business. Yes.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yes, there was a lot of business.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: You know, I want to ask you some about Potlatch. I was a little bit worried about the housing and living there. Now Gus told me that the housing for people that had come from Eastern Europe was different, a separate place, not in the main part of town, not where the bigger houses were. Oh no, different.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: You know, Potlatch, you know, was a common town, you understand?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Everything was owned by a common store. All the big shots they were living up to the high prices. Knob Hill there. And them houses where Gus lived now, they built them up. I don't remember.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: They had down the flat, you know, down the flat, right close, pretty close, found down there, they throw the logs there. They had a company building, build a big, fresh, 14 or 15 rooms, you know, long. And they had put petition and you got to live for four or five people. They had two bedrooms, you know, kitchen. That's all kitchen. And we had two bedrooms. And after that, they turned down them, you know, they always start fire down there. Yeah, there was a fire.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: How did that, did you know about that fire?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Until they got a fire down there, then they built up them houses up there where Gus live now. Down the street from the coast of the state, you know. Gus told me it was some kid that didn't know, didn't know about the fire, that started the fire by mistake. He dumped out the ashes or something. All them kids, of course, they can do anything as far as that goes, you know. I don't know any better, you know. They dump something outside, you know, they start fire. Especially, you know, them houses down there, you know, they build out of their lumber.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Well, did the guys mind living in a place like that that was crowded and not kind of separated off? Do they mind?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: It doesn't sound too good to me at that time. They should have had a separate, separated the people like that. Well, they had different places for the Greeks, and different places for Italians, and different places for Japanese. And they, you know, and Swedes too. The Ximix people, you know. But we had one section, all Greeks. We stayed, you know, on that long building, yes, and they all divided up in the rooms. Leave all Greeks, no Italians, no Japanese.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Did the guys like that? Is that what you guys wanted?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: No, nothing you can do, nothing else to do, you know. You got to stay some place. They didn't have anything else to go and stay. You can go up the Hanoi, you know, but too far to go back and forth, you know. You can go up the Hanoi, you can find little places up there. But hell, it's too far, you know. But them rooms, they come down there when they build up that big room. They have houses, big buildings, you know, one building. I don't know how many rooms. They have nice rooms, you know, two nice bedrooms and one room, you know. For a court, you know, they sit down and talk to you. It was pretty nice, nice, we keep nice and clean. Every Sunday, you know, we feel the blood, we wash the blood, you know, everything.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Was there a place to meet besides in the houses?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: To meet, you see. To meet, to get together. We got together today, I said, tonight we'll go to your vision, on your house. Next day, on my house. It was before people leave each house.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: So what did you do in your spare time when you wasn't working then? Did you gamble much?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: We do. We are gambling.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: We play little phones, no?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: We play little dials.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: A little what?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Dials.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: We play cards, you know, like all country.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: All country games?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yes, you know. So that's how we pass the time. And sometimes we get together, you know, and we used to know somebody. He was an engineer, he was rolling the lock. A train who carried locks from Bovill to Potlatch. He had some friends or relations at Spokane. He always go to Spokane to vision. Every two weeks, you know, Saturday night. Then come back Sunday night. We go together, stay overnight in Spokane. That's the day. Then come Monday morning. Back to work. There was still quite a bit of booze too in Potlatch, even though they didn't supposed to have it.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Oh, you mean a bootlegger?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yeah, the bootlegger. Oh hell, of course. Any place you go, especially down here, you know, there's lots of bootleggers. But in the Potlatch, there was some Italian, you know. They was making some wine. Grapes wine. Oh, my God.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: From... They was making wine.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Grapes, you know, dry grapes.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: The raisins?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yeah, and they was making wine. And we go and get together. They know each other. The Italians and the ... mix them up. They were not scared, you know, for us. Always we go visit them, you know, and they always give us drinks. We buy drinks, you know, and we had a lot of fun, just to say. It's a long time we play poker, you know, together, you know. We was getting a long time. We had no troubles, you know. So they spent time together in two groups.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: What about mixing with the local townspeople?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Oh, that's easy, you know. We mix down and go to work, you know. See, if you work out the mill, it's all kinds of people. Because in the sawmill, most of it was all bricks.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: In the sawmill?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yeah, in the sawmill. And some of these American people. Or down outside in a plenary or a box factory, you know. There was all kinds. Docs mix people. It's easy to get acquainted with in a small town like a potluck, you know. When we see each other every day, we go to work every day, you know. And every morning and every noon time we go home and eat, you know. Where Guss live now, you know. Well, noon time we go and eat dinner. Go home to bed. Yeah, and go to bed to work at one o 'clock in the evening. So we meet all of us together. Especially, you know, with the Swedes. Italians and Greeks. We get together, play, you know, cards. Drink sometimes. But not to get drunk, no, no. No problems, no problems.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Who was getting drunk?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And the only... There was some, right across from the West, was a very big building. There was boarding house. Then it was all Japanese. Most of the Japanese live in there. And the only one Japanese, he was associated with us because he was a gaman, that fella. And he liked to gaman, see. He was often nice guy, son of a bitch, you know. And we have a lot of money for him to eat. He don't like to stay good, his people. He like to come and associate with us, you know.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: But the others pretty much, they stayed together. Yes, yes, yes. Only one guy, that guy, he was awful like man. And he liked us, we like him too, you know. Especially we play, you know. He don't drink much, he don't drink. And we play, he play, you know, nice, clean, everything. Now I tell you something, my friend. He left that giant and he went to Japan. It's all country.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So friend mine and I, I told him, Jimmy, when you go back... He's my Jimmy. When you go back, all of country, we give you money. You gonna send us three yards of material that is... That time, you know, material for... Shield material, you know. To make a sword, you know.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: To make what, a sword?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Short, short, to make a sword. We know some Japanese woman, she can do it, you know. Yes, yes, yes, she says, yes.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And we says, how much money you want?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Three dollars you, three dollars you, not too much. We send you three yards of shield material, so you can make two short circle, one to shy. Now, that guy, he went back to country, my friend, he did send us.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: He did?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: He did. Send us the material.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Are you thinking about that?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: That's good. Yes, sure.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Didn't you think he would?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Sure. We had confidence because he was good man, you know. He was borrowing money lots of times for us, you know, when we played. He like to play some money and borrow. Pay they come, he pay. Sometimes we borrow for him, we pay him, you know, them days, anything. Them days, you know, was different. If you got a friend, you had a friend. These days, no, you got no friend these days. These days, your friend is this, the green stock. You got the green stock, you got a friend. No green stock, no friend. Yes, he went back to country and he sent us the material. In them days, it did not matter if you had money, didn't have nothing to do with being a friend. No. Money didn't depend on... Them days, for the friendship, I tell you this.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: No.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: We know each other, we work together there for a long time. And sometimes you need a little money. Ama, he said, twenty or thirty dollars.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: And you say, well, can I borrow thirty dollars in a pay day?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: I want to do something or I want to send the money. And next time, you know, you can borrow thirty dollars. Next pay the money, I return money. We don't say no, we say yes. Pay the money, he pay back. These days you loan, you know, I've got a good friend and you loan him a dollar, Ama, he says. You never give him back. If you go down the street and he see, you come down, he cross the street, he go the other side. Them days was different, my friend, these days such a difference. Them days you got a friend and if you ask him for fifty dollars, he give it to you, but he give him back. These days you can't. These days people, they don't care. They never return, they loan somebody two dollars. I tell you what. There was friend here, he was an Italian fella, now he's coaching with the American Legion basketball. And he come one day and he almost come, he get a shot, and he one day he come and says, You know I have two dollars today, and I give it to you after tomorrow, after a week or two, and she say, I give it to you. Finally he give it to me back, he return, he give him back. Now they loan for two, something like that, I don't know, he come back again and he was short. Really morning, them days you know, two dollars, you can go anywhere, but now you can. I give it to him, two dollars, because I gave him the first time and he return, you see. So I say, his credit is good, so next time when he come, I give him two dollars, he never come back. I tell him to come back again, I don't know, three, four months. He can't figure up, you know, he get a shot. I never ask him. But the two dollars I give him, I say, I didn't give it to him. He never did offer, he never say anything. I got no money, I know I owe you two dollars, I might pay you later on, something like that. I never say money. I say, I have him of course, you know. And he might be someday, he return, if he return or if he don't know what he have, he come back again. Well sir, in the long run, I don't know, he return or two.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: He come down one day, got a shot and he paid me for the shot and he says, can I borrow two dollars Mike and I give it to you next week.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So I call him on. No.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Because you give me, you borrow two dollars at the first time and you return. Second time, I says to him, you come back and I give you two dollars and I lost you for cost to me too. First time you came here now after five, six months and now you want me to give you two dollars. You think I forget the two dollars I give you, I don't know. I don't forget. Now I don't care either.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So, where are the people?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Some people are right, some people are not. Down the path like you know, who has worked in the path like you know, we know, we all together. You can borrow money you know, it's like somebody need a little money every payday and everything.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And he gave me twenty dollars, some like that, twenty fifty dollars, he send back four quarters you know. Payday come, he come and he pay back but you can't get him now anymore. Twenty five dollars, that's quite a bit of money to loan in that days. Damn days.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Of your friends at Potlatch, were most of your close friends, were most of them Greeks?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: They were the people that you spend most of your time with. Because we all lives together. Not in one house, you know the house is now. Gus and I was living together. I don't know. Gus he had a house by himself, he and he had a brother. And he brother, he went, he have wife. So he stay alone. There was but three four houses above where the house is now. We are similar you know.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And then we had a house right next to Gus. And we as four of us stayed there. There was three from his own town and myself. It was getting long fine you know. So after I brother go, he asked us if we had no problem to come and stay with us. And we said yes. We come, there was four, five of us. We have two bedrooms in one room.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Is that the house Gus has got now?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: No, that was right next to him. Next to him, yeah that's right. I remember he told me that used to be his house. Yeah, right next to him. Then he moved in that house which is now. Up next to him you know, he borrowed from the company. And company sell the house and he bought it. He went back to the old country for a visit. But I guess he had notion to stay. You know back to old country. But he find the things different. You know.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: He had bought himself a new car. I guess. Thirty five hundred dollars something like that. And I go back to old country. Sold out and put it here. Two thousand dollars something like that. He lost money. Because he think around and go back there and stay with him. He went back there you know. He find different movie. So he don't like it and he come back. He come back broke. Good thing. He told me when he came. I went in a visit to him you know. And I asked him how you like the old country. If you meet the fella who are stay together. He was from the same town with the Gus you know. And they find out I was coming. They come you know and meet me. When I get out the bus you know. But they keep me to home and poor you know. And he says. I tell you he says. That's alright. He says. To go back there and stay. Because you got a little money. And stay away from your relationships. You be better off you know. They needed. They were asking him for money back there. Absolutely. Well they didn't die. He says he got a sister and a nephew back there. And of course he was living in a hotel. In Athens. Of course he was go to place where he born you know. That's a long way from. In Dyneba. Yeah Dyneba. So he went there and visit the people where he went. And he don't stay very long or he will be blessed. And he come back and he went to Athens. And he went back to his Dyneba again. In Easter Sunday you know. And back there in Easter Sunday you know. They celebrate you know. They got lambs, roast lambs you know. Barbecue lambs you know. And they got some music, dance and sing. You know, he got a lot of phone you know. And he joined that burial or anything else. See. Yeah he told me. When he came I went and see him. Well he had a nephew. He was working there some place. He was working for government and he got a sister there. They had apartment.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Gus he was stay in the hotel.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So his nephew, he asked him to go to stay with him in the hotel. He don't like to stay with him. Because Gus he had lots of burial room you know. You know Gus. He spent money. He like to live like a man you know. He is not stingy or anything like that. He is a good man.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: So he says, well what I'm gonna do?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So I told him to come and stay with him. Pretty soon he says.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: He but, he went and he rented the... ...motor.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: For foolish apartment. He got apartment. Foolish with everything. He left it like the big arm of a hidden umbrella. For seventy dollars a month. And then you got a paper. United States money. Money. Dollars. And pay anyone a motor and then too.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yeah he told me.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well... I give them money. They are not satisfied and they want more. I go down in restaurant you know. We get together he says nephew and this and that. They are relationship. Then to have together you know. I pay everything. They cost money.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And give me, give me a little time. The last time he says, not tell you the truth, he says, I was broke. Good thing he says I had my ticket round trip.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: He was... When he go back there... He was going to stay.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: But he had quite a good morning. And he was to operate the social security. He sold his car. He sold that house. He was next to him you know. And when he come back he went to Moscow and from Moscow he went and he bought that house. And we live now.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: How many years ago was that? Do you remember? About what year?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: No I can remember.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Was that in the 50's or in the 60's?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Some like that. Because it would probably... Just after he retired. Soon after he retired. He retired. You know and then. Even before he retired. So it must have been about 15 to 20 years ago. Yes. Some like that. Maybe 15 years ago. Well he says, oh hell he says, good thing he says I got the ticket round trip. I had not much money with me to come back. But good thing he had some... Bounce you know. He bounce you know. And he had a weapon. And he went at the United States Consul in Athens. And he give it to him and he send back over here in the cash. He keep the money and he come back. I said no more. No I don't know. I don't know what to say. Because anytime you write something it says Kim Jim. Like my nephew. They think we are rich because we've been here in this country a long time ago. They came with their money by the bush. They say Jim Jim send me, send me. I send them you know. I got the nephew. I send them every Christmas. I send 50 dollars to the same Christmas. That's all. No connection. No letters no more. Only one. Very good enough. They ask me to go back there. To stay with them. We take care of you. I says to them, I wrote to one fellow and I told him I know what kind of care you gonna take.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: You wanna take me or you wanna this guy?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: That brings stuff.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: When you were first here at Potlatch did you send money back to your family then?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yes. I sent money back to my family. Yes. I did send lots of money too.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Did you send regular? Every paycheck?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: No no every paycheck.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: I sent only 5 dollars, 30 dollars, 50 dollars. Only one time. That was 19, 20, 19, 18 or 19, 19 something like that. I had a sister back then. Then she was old enough to get married. And back there you know they got a bad assistant. You know if you got a daughter you got married you know. You gotta give me something. I gave money or property to him you know to marry your daughter. It's not like here but now it's like over here. They get together and love each other you know. But them there you know you got a daughter and somebody come after. They wanna have her or something too. A thousand, darkness, two thousand, five thousand. You got to have some land you know. Something all the time they like to give me give me something. So my father wrote me letter and he told me we find a good boy and he's well to do. And we figure out to marry your sister on such a day. Such a month. So we need 500 dollars. 500 dollars you know there was a lot of money.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: But I had little money and it been safe you know. I had it in the bank you know.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And I went and I got a draft cheque from Potlatch State in Paris.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: From who?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: State Potlatch State that used to be there. Of course it was all company town everything you know company. They run all by the warehouses you know. Okay.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So I got a cheque and I got a cheque or her name. Not of my father you know or hen or her name. Her name. But I'm sure you remember I don't know if you are young I don't know who had the flu. 1918 big flu you know a lot of people. By the time I sent the letter you know my sister's gone. She was, she died. She had the flu you know. She and my father together but father didn't find any drug. It got well and my sister is dying. Now the cheque was on her name. The Ken Kashi man. See. So they sent my cheque back.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well I was a little bit upset that I disappointed myself.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: You were upset?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: My sister died. Yeah.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So I said to myself now I better go down the bed and I told this cheque over to my father never. Long as I send him this cheque to go my sister sent him back. I got it. So I sent my old man and him. And drugs you know. Need a little money. He was sick you know during that year. Much sick. But he was down here.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And that's when they go.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: But that then who's now at the puddle I see. I think is now Idaho First Nation then. I think it is. Yeah. Or First Security. I'm not sure. I don't know if it's First Security or Idaho First Nation. I believe it is Idaho Nation. Idaho band. Because that's a part of the business with Idaho First Nation. You know I was going to ask you about the women. It seems like it was all bachelors or men. Some men maybe had wives back in the old country. But there were no women living in Potlach from Greece. No. Yeah. So me I married guys. You know. They had families back home. But see they make a little money and they send a little money every month. Or every weekend. 15, 20 dollars. It was pretty good money then. But then. But the women was trying to keep his family alive. And for the single guys.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: It was different. But guys was all men. All masculine guys. We are single. And after the military down in 1932. Some guys you know. From Boston town. They were in the corresponding. Some of the guys went back there. Did all the guys. Did the guys that were married. And had their wives back there. They all go back. Not all of them. Some stayed. Some stayed. Some stayed. Some guys I know. When we lived together. I know some of them. Some of them I don't know. You can tell these days. People stayed. But some of them did go. During the depression over here. The early morning. Everything shut down. They didn't job no place. So they decided and they go back also. And somebody they come back.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: During the World War II.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: The German. Was. Took over the Greece. German and Italian. And some guys. On the left of this corner. If you went back there. There was American citizen. So. They come back. They were in the Greek Consulate. You know in the happiness. And they. Show them there was American citizen and everything. So. Even they didn't have no money. But they give them the ticket and they come. When they got a job. They pay the ticket. To the government. Because government. Send the money. Well you take. This. The men that had the wives back in Greece. Did that make their families. Pretty well off by them sending back money. Did it make a big difference. At the time. Well at the time. Yes. If you got little money you know. You got a. Eat little better. You got a dress up little better you know. And if you have no money. You know how they give. It is. See. Like you or me. If you got any money. You see something you like. You buy. If you got no money. You see something you like. You can buy. You got no money. Or. Same thing you go to the store. Or in a butcher shop. Or in a pleasure shop. If you got no money. You see something you like. You can buy. You can buy. You look at the other. Same thing you got no money. Well you. Like. Take like you and your friends. The young fellas that didn't have. That weren't married. You guys didn't have much of a chance to. To. You know marry over here.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Did you?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well yes. You got the change. I had a change. Two or three changes. But. Can get. Long. You can satisfy.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: These girls these days. I had a change. To marry couple American girls. I had a change to marry one. Miracle widow. I was. I don't know.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Am I treating me too good? You might what?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Treating me too good. Now they say treat me rough. They like you better.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: You treating me good?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: No.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So. Decide my sister.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Then I said. Now I am gonna stay single.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And. Love them. And live them. This. I got married. Good girls. Young girls. Is already married. But used to send they don't care if they are married or not. These days. Marry them. Seems to me. Marry life. They don't mean anything. This young girl these days. They cannot change. Especially if you talk to somebody else. And if you know a little bit. BS. They lay down. Well in those days. Wasn't there lots of. Wasn't there lots of prostitution. Lots of whore houses like in. Yes. Here too. Houston too. Yes. No more sending a war worker too. Come they. But you find lots of girls. Lots of girls. Young girls. No. Men girls. They come after you. I don't know. What. When it become a discontinue. Young girls you know. They like to step out. Marry them. Young girls. Twenty two. Twenty four. Twenty seven. Twenty eight. But it didn't used to be like that in the old days. No no. No here no. You see during the depression my friend. We was different. People then you know. They didn't know money. They were hungry. They are not like this. Now we are on depression. You know take off. Cut them off this warfare and. Social security. You know that itself. You know. But this warfare and social security. They say. You know to go home. They might eat once or two each a day. But they don't go home. By them days they didn't have money. We go from Potlatch. We used to go to Spokane. Saturday night. And you see if you go to L 'Ebleche. 10th or 11th club. You see all the girls. You can take them home for all night. Stay for all night for thirty dollars. For thirty dollars. But. That was different because there was. A hard time. And so the girls. Had to do something. Had to make a living.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Were some of them really just nice people? Those girls? Some of them just good people?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well you don't know. If there was a good people or not. It's pretty hard to tell. But now. I tell you this now. For my time. I don't trust them. If I don't know the girl real well. I don't trust them. I don't. I know. Oh no. Long time ago. No but two three weeks ago. Long time after long time. I woke up top. Up the street you know. That's a girl coming right. Outside there. Good looking girl. Good girl. It was. Well dressed up you know. She had nice.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Pen suit you know. She was looking for you. And she don't know me. I see her. She sitting on the. Full horse down there. And she. The girl. The lonesome time. Sometimes she got a friend. And I was. I never really talked to her before. And she says. How about you give me. Twenty dollars and I pay to you. Pay day.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: She says what?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: She says you give me twenty dollars.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Can I borrow twenty?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: She says can I borrow twenty dollars from you. And I give it to you pay day.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: I says. My boss he didn't pay me.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Honest to God. I says to. My boss he didn't pay me.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: That other day. And she. She come in here once.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And one day he came and she was drawn. Son of a.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And she says Mike. I see her. You can go pretty close to that. Smell like a bird.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: I said now what do you want?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: She says. You know me. I come here here. She says yeah I know you. You come here here. She says five dollars. I need five dollars and I pay to you. Sometimes next week. I told my, I tell you my. No pay day yet. No pay day. Pay day no count. That's it. Boss he says no money no pay day. Such a different you know. Different ending. You know I wanted to ask you.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Do you remember the IWWs?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yes. Them we were the guys who bring these fellas. And bring these hours down to eight hours a day. And more money. Them was the first guys in the IWW. I've heard they were. That they were really a good good union. But also I've heard. I've heard that they didn't get too far in potlatch in the town. No. They were mostly in the woods. Yes. They start from the woods. Because you know. Afterwards. Was working long hours. No shower. No clean beds you know. Rehear like the hooks. That's where they started you see. Now you go afterwards you know. Everything compare. They got it like very like you go stay in the devil's house. Good food. Good room. Good bed. Shower and everything. More money. Short hours. Overtime double time. But then they know. Now that WW they do that.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: In the mill did many of the guys join?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: No we never did have that guy in the mill.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Never got the cards in the mill?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: No no only in the mill we had the. Four air no. Four air. Yeah I thought that was a company's union. Yes. Yes that was company union.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: That didn't do anything for the men?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: No. Sometimes you know we ask for more money. They say that the union men come from. Holland while the headquarters you know in Poland. We get together you know. They had a whole lot of. I don't know maybe they still got the whole lot there. But that story is now you know. That then. They had a whole lot still you know. Then they say they search a day. Search night. Seven o 'clock. Break everywhere. They could be. The union meeting. Father from Poland come. Gonna give us real space. And general manager gonna give real space. Where all the people you know they work. And we'll go there. Less not there they gonna search. And see if they can give us. Little more money you know.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Where they start?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well they started in the fine exclusion. And says general manager. You fellas have enough money but you don't. You spend it. Yeah that's what she said. You spend that money thousand dollars in a chugam. That much thousand dollars in there. They have everything in there. They sit down you know. You see. They find some excuse not to give us little rest you know.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: No share. The union was working. It was his union. Yes. That's not what a union is supposed to do. We was paid dollar more. Fifty different. I don't know.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Them people there was a camp pretty close during the depression you know. There was not much a job. A lot of them white people you know was out to work. And they like to give in to take us.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Who did?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Them American people you know. They gonna have a job you know. And they try to take us boys out of the job and to give in themselves you know. You see. Yeah.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So Mr. Larry you know. They call up union. Who are in need of your life. In that hole you know. Well everybody come. He says to the manager he want give good speech tonight. To everybody.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So we went then. Then he started talking and he says now you fellas complain about the Greeks working. The Italians working. The Japanese working. The Soviet working. And you fellas stay out of the world. You gonna have a job. Now. Now. Now he says you had a job there. You was working there. You make a little money and you quit your job and you went in a different place to get a job. And you can find a job and you come back. And that fella who's working in his home in the Greeks there. They been working there for a long time. And that we can get them out of there and put you. Or you or you. You had a job and you left it. You go in a different place to get another job or get more money. Now you come back here and you like us to take them Greeks off. Or the Italian off. Or the Japanese off them job and put you house. That's impossible. We can't do that. Because time for who we need. Them boys we have right in the job. But you don't stick on the job. You fellas like to fool around. But them guys they deep. Stick on the job and we have. And we can do nothing for you fellas. Boy you have a nice man. No more complain or nothing.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: You see. Were there other times. There are other times that you can remember that they discriminated or tried to discriminate against the Italians and the Greeks and other people. No no no. They didn't have anything like that. No discrimination. I heard. I think Gus said to me that. That you guys didn't feel like you could use the gym too much. You didn't have much to do with the athletic. Well no yes we never did know. Yes we never did. Because we were like you. We didn't have no time. We don't know much you know. English to speak you know. And we flew around ourselves in the evening in the top you know. We would play cards and this and that. And we didn't have time you know to go in the gym. Now taking is humble you know. We were working in the home there. You can hear. You can talk. One person to the other you know. If you do you like to go and talk right or easy. It goes too much you know. You can hear nothing there. It's only. If you are somebody. To ask you or you ask him. What time is it you know. Day. Well if it's ten o 'clock. He says. Like it is. That means ten o 'clock. Shaking the hands. Yeah. Fingers like it is. That means ten o 'clock. This is down. Means eleven. Two fingers down. Yeah eleven. Then this way. To help. This way. Close hands. Yeah yeah. Thumb. Close you hear me like this. Fingers too you know. Yeah yeah yeah. To help. Or we're core. My core. Nine. Eight. That way. That way. Seven. That way. Eight. That way.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Nine. I don't remember nine.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Nine was one finger down. Seven this way. Eight this way. Nine that way. Now core. If it's. Core to ten. After ten. This is. Core. After. Ten. Usabi. Or core to ten. Core. Before. That's where it was. Talking inside them.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Could you say other things besides the time? Could you talk to people, have a conversation? Using your hands?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Oh sure you can go working you know. Like I told you it's only conversation you got to ask somebody time. Or if you want to go out and call it and you want somebody coming in chat like you play yourself. Do a circle on your stomach.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: See. See. There are lots of things. When you work you know in the mill especially you got too much noise you can hear anything.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Was the mill work, was working in the mill hard? And was it the work hard to do?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Oh that days was pretty easy. These days pretty hard. They change them all around you know.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: You didn't have to work too fast or nothing in the mill?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: No.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: The work wasn't no then? The work wasn't too fast?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: No. Sometimes you do you know it depends you know how the lumber coming you know. Some places you got to work pretty fast, some places pretty slow. But they didn't mean much here because the lumber coming you know was only one inch. And boring you know. Sometimes when he was getting old you know for five or six quarters or eight quarters. He's only little hot job while he's taking his timber out of the red for you know. Only maybe four by six or six by six or six by eight or something like that. Otherwise the rest of it was tripped. Them days it was pretty easy job. And that mill there was like a school you know. You can go around any place. Now you can. You could look and see though how it's done. Yes, yes. But you know.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: You weren't held in one place then? You weren't held in one place? You could move around?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Oh yeah sure you can move around. I'm old.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Listen now was it harder? Did a Greek person or an Italian person have to work? Was it harder for him to get ahead than it was for an American in the mill? Could you advance as easy as an American could? Or did it take more?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well in the mill you know they got different jobs. Not very hard. But on Italians you know all them Italians they work outside the mill you know. No longer.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: The green chain?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Green chain. Or dry keel or planer you know. For them it's a hard job you know. Pretty heavy. But no, no. Not inside the mill though. Inside the mill was not a job.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: How come they were outside?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: The Italians. Well they had them outside. I mean they didn't get a job outside. They can get in in the mill you know. Because like I did. Gus did. We had a job there. We stayed there for 15, 20 years you know. So no change for anybody else to get in you know. But outside they can get in.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Were most of the Greek people inside the mill?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: No.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: They were inside?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: All inside. Nobody was outside. Only one guy, one Greek fella, they moved from somewhere. They took him down in a replander. And he was rolling the pool saw, the trimming, the lumber. The trimming, the dressing lumber you know. He cut the four feet or two feet you know. On his truck or big nuts you know. All the Greeks was working inside the mill. Nobody was outside.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: So then you really had the better jobs than some of the people?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Oh yes. We had a better job. Oh yes we had. And we had a good phone with Mike then too.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Who is that?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: John Meyer.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Do you like the Greeks?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Because you know he had somebody on his job.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: See. Any morning when we should blow 7 o 'clock. He go up and down. He look around. He see everybody on his press. See. And he didn't worry or anything. Well sometimes it's our nights shift. He didn't got no people to work. Well he come and he says, Mike you only go tonight in such place because we shot again. I didn't say no. I go working. No overtime. Straight time.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Some different job call the other color. So we help both each sometimes. Not all of them but sometimes with the short time.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So that's where we have a nice common. And you know one time. That was Christmas time. Sometimes it's only really below zero. You know those many difference if it's Christmas or New Year or things giving that meal you gotta go. Here on shut down because if you do next day when they start you see them cherries busting. Because it's a frost see.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: What would bust?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: The chance. The change.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So one it's below zero. One time was Christmas.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So in the middle of one morning he got a block board you know. Here for one of the stairwells they get up off the top of the mill you know. And everybody go take his place. He got a block board and he. And he write all the orders he got you know. All the orders he got. For long man and they got a cut and a ship him out. He get out of the mill and go down the green chain you know and take him out. Go there and he says a meal will run tomorrow. That was Christmas day.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So one fella. He did come Christmas.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: He stay home. We have to ask the foreman. Because you know don't do him any good to ask him because. He don't let him. He need him to be there.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: All of us we got. And you know he didn't come to work because it was Christmas and his name was Chris. And all of us Greeks you know. We celebrate the name. Celebrating. Here they celebrate their birthday. But we celebrate the name day.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: His name was Chris?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yes. Because it was Christmas. And he don't want to work that day. Because we celebrate the Christmas. His name was Chris.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: So foreman was coming we signed down there you know. He look down there he was working a trimmer you know. And one of us fella they motioned the boss. He say so one man short of the hill. This fella. I have been sit at boss. I do myself nice home. Well he said somebody else took his place.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well took his place. Next day this Chris he come to work.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: He come to work. Foreman he see him he was on the job. We get up we go there. Good morning Chris. He was like that. Good morning John.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: What's tomorrow you yesterday? What you doing? When did you come to work yesterday?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Oh he says I didn't feel very good. She got excuse. Don't feel very good no. Well you better go home and stay one week to be well and come back next week. Then we came home.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: He could have canned him. Yeah but he run to can him. Because he's been working a long time ago you know. But you get the penalty. Well he says you don't feel very good you better go home. Stay another week you'll get to work and come back Monday.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well if you and the American guy wanted the same job in the mill. We get long fine. But say you both wanted the same job for instance. You wanted the same job.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Would he have a better chance to get it?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: If I quit yes. Or if he quit I take it. Because I know. Because I used to work for him you know. Sometimes we change each other in the jobs you know.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: You understand?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Because I like a lot of that job you got. So he give me change. He says okay I change with you. You have now. That means you have now. See. And he like to come on my job because my job actually get more lighter or he get job. But I like to learn. He says yes. And if it happen and he quit I got a change to get the job. You see. Like that. But no. Now they wait.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: We get long fine. Whenever we get have no argument we never get have a fine. No. We get long fine. Very nice. No arguments. No hard feelings. No nothing. We are very good friends.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Did you guys joke around much?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Oh hell yes. I heard you.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: You play jokes on each other?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Oh yes.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Oh yes. We was getting long fine. We had no arguments. No hard feelings. No no no. We mix you know sometimes. Sometimes Sandy you know we had some trees. Still they got them trees outside when the Gus is. Well we shoot the dyes down the ground you know. They get mix of Greeks, Americans, Jews, Jews, and Japanese. Play dyes. Down the ground.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Hey could a guy lose his whole paycheck?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yes. Yes. Oh yes sure. You bet.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Oh yeah.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Yes sure.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Did most of those of your friends did they stay for a long time in Potlatch or did they was there a lot of turnover? Did many of them many of the Greeks leave and go back to Greece or go someplace else?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: The guys who left and go back to Greece they went back to the Mealshedon in 1932, 1931 in October. Of course. Because they didn't get no place, they didn't get no job you know and if they stay or they couldn't spend no everything and only they got. Still getting a little older and after that they get a job. So they left and went back home.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Did it take most people to take the long time for them to learn and speak English good?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well I tell you. Now if you are mixed with these people all the time you can learn English quick. And if you work in the mill like you've been with for a long time you know you can speak with your friend you know like I told you you got all the signs you know you can learn you know any language you can learn nothing you got a mix you know to learn the language. Otherwise you can't. Like inside the mill you can hear nothing only with the signs. Except after we quit working you know we go and even we go to the store you know or walk up the street all together you know. Yeah we talk a little English but you can learn English you know if you don't mix. You see. Right.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: So when did you get a chance to learn English?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Well I know how you mix. I got a good mixer with the people.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: So you learn like you learn more when you have to come down here?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Because here all the time you know you got to do business with these people. They speak English. So you can come a little bit at a time a little bit here a little bit there. Where you're speaking this all the time. But if you work in like I said in the mill you know you can learn anything you know like yourself.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Did most guys save, could they save money?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: They did. They did yes.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: They could put some away?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Some guys it was this one. I mean time. They don't spend money they save. And sometimes like a Gus. I. We little too loose we spend money you know. Money for us didn't mean anything. But remember this my friend. We never stay anything. English will be alright. Today we got started up like we had you know. We know that. We want to spoken. One day we might get in a game you know playing diamonds. We might lose $100 or $150. We never think that. Because we know when we go back next day we got a job. We're good. But we never look ahead though. Like in like in Millie's shut down and most of us we was broke.

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: You see the point?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: And them fellas who was this they don't go no player they had the money. That's it.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Did you miss Potlatch when you left it?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: Oh yeah.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Come down here?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
words: No. Because I know I'm not going to go back there. Because I was not there. And after coming down here I made lots of friends you know. Looks mixed here. A lot of your friends left there too. All them friends left you know. It was only Gus. He was there and not a guy. And then he passed away three or four years ago and the Holy Gus was alone there.

speaker: Sam Schrager
words: Didn't Potlatch didn't like they didn't treat the single men the same way they did the married people did they? During the Depression they tried to help out the married people some at Potlatch didn't they?

speaker: Mike Stefanos
